hypothesis: The Jepsen architecture-layer → determinism gradient (L-642: state-machine > protocol-spec > client-server > distributed-clock) predicts swarm-internal distributed failure modes. Classification accuracy >70%. Swarm shows higher overall determinism than databases due to git's strong consistency.
methodology: Catalog 24 swarm-internal distributed failures from lessons (L-145 through L-666) and MEMORY.md. Classify each by: (1) Jepsen architecture layer, (2) determinism type, (3) minimum node/session count, (4) detection method, (5) fix type. Test gradient correlation. Compare with F95 Jepsen bug classifications (n=5).

taxonomy.note: Fifth layer (infrastructure) discovered during classification — Jepsen tests software bugs, not substrate failures. Swarm uniquely exposes this layer via WSL filesystem corruption.

classifications.name: Cross-reference count drift
classifications.determinism_detail: Any session that updates without verifying against source of truth causes drift. 100% reproducible.
classifications.jepsen_analog: none — silent state accumulation, not crash/partition bug

classifications.name: FRONTIER NEAR-RESOLVED stale status
classifications.determinism_detail: NEAR-RESOLVED is snapshot from naming session, not live. Concurrent completion without FRONTIER update always causes this.
classifications.jepsen_analog: none — semantic staleness, not data loss

classifications.name: Minimal contract violation → distinct failures
classifications.determinism_detail: Each of 5 invariant components produces specific, predictable failure mode when removed.
classifications.jepsen_analog: Redis-Raft #14 (state-machine re-entrancy — systematic logic error, 100% repro)

classifications.name: Append-only log windup
classifications.determinism_detail: Append-only with closure events deterministically bloats. 3.72x bloat ratio measured.
classifications.jepsen_analog: none — log growth, not correctness bug

classifications.name: Substrate coupling in entry commands
classifications.determinism_detail: Entry command assumes swarm repo structure. Always fails on foreign repos.
classifications.jepsen_analog: none — portability, not distributed failure

classifications.name: Scope-limited Sharpe misclassification
classifications.determinism_detail: grep scope didn't include all directories. Deterministic logic error.
classifications.jepsen_analog: none — analysis tool bug

classifications.name: Age-normalized Sharpe cannot distinguish absorbed/orphaned
classifications.determinism_detail: Algorithm design limitation — temporal adjustment cannot distinguish retirement from loss.
classifications.jepsen_analog: none — classification limitation

classifications.name: Simpson's paradox in genesis selection
classifications.determinism_detail: Observational confound from correlated session allocation. Deterministic given data.
classifications.jepsen_analog: none — measurement confound

classifications.name: Concurrent top-score convergence
classifications.determinism_detail: Multiple sessions reading same state always select same top-scored item. Deterministic given concurrent read of same state.
classifications.jepsen_analog: Redis-Raft #17 (protocol membership — deterministic given partition sequence)

classifications.name: DUE-item convergence
classifications.determinism_detail: Greedy top-pick selection converges when sessions see same global state. 37.5% commit overhead at N≥3.
classifications.detection: duplicate DUE edits in git log
classifications.jepsen_analog: Redis-Raft #17 (protocol gap — no deduplication in membership changes)

classifications.name: Logical overwrite (last-writer-wins)
classifications.determinism_detail: Git does not prevent sequential overwrites to same path. Last writer always wins. Deterministic given commit sequence.
classifications.detection: git diff between commits; content comparison
classifications.fix_type: two-layer guard (P-081 density + check HEAD:path before write)
classifications.jepsen_analog: Redis-Raft #19 (stale reads — missing protocol step allows incorrect state)

classifications.name: Lane-closure orphaning
classifications.determinism_detail: When work diffuses across sessions, no single session owns closing ritual. Deterministic given concurrent work pattern.
classifications.jepsen_analog: none — ownership diffusion, novel to multi-agent

classifications.name: Orient→execute pre-emption
classifications.determinism_detail: Planning becomes obsolete during orient phase. All planned tasks pre-empted at N≥3. Timing-dependent: commit rate exceeds planning cycle.
classifications.detection: git log shows task completed during planning
classifications.fix_type: re-check git log before each task; switch to novel work
classifications.jepsen_analog: etcd #11456 (client-server race — wide timing window)

classifications.name: Shared-file write loss
classifications.determinism_detail: Lock-free append under N=2-4 lost 12/1040 writes. Fixed by lock-file protocol to 0 loss.
classifications.jepsen_analog: etcd #11456 (race condition in client library, wide window)

classifications.name: Lesson-slot contention
classifications.determinism_detail: Two sessions independently choose same lesson number. 8 events at N≥5.
classifications.threshold_behavior: N<5: rare; N≥5: frequent (8 events observed)
classifications.jepsen_analog: CockroachDB #9083 (concurrent timestamp collision)

classifications.name: Hot-file contention
classifications.determinism_detail: Concurrent sessions converge on same hot files (PAPER.md, INDEX.md, FRONTIER.md). Orient.py output stale before execution.
classifications.threshold_behavior: grows linearly with N; systematic at N≥3
classifications.fix_type: verify periodics.json directly; file-level conflict avoidance
classifications.jepsen_analog: etcd #11456 (concurrent access to shared resource)

classifications.name: Staged files absorbed by concurrent commits (N≥8)
classifications.determinism_detail: At N≥8, staged files get absorbed/emptied by concurrent commits. Small fast commits work; large batches don't.
classifications.detection: empty staged files after commit attempt
classifications.jepsen_analog: none — unique to shared working tree (databases don't share staging)

classifications.name: Commit-by-proxy absorption (N≥5)
classifications.determinism_detail: Concurrent nodes harvest uncommitted working tree files into their handoff commits. Normal at high concurrency.
classifications.detection: git log shows content in another session's commit
classifications.fix_type: check git log before re-doing; accepted as normal
classifications.jepsen_analog: none — unique to shared working tree; more like gossip protocol than bug

classifications.name: Claim TTL ghost lock
classifications.determinism_detail: TTL calibrated for single-session (300s) creates 5-cycle overlap at N≥5. Depends on session count vs TTL ratio.
classifications.threshold_behavior: ghost_cycles = ceil(TTL / commit_cycle) - 1; at 300s/60s = 4 extra cycles
classifications.detection: session reports claim held by expired session
classifications.fix_type: reduce TTL 300s→120s + GC before claim + release on git-add
classifications.jepsen_analog: CockroachDB #9083 (distributed-clock: timing mismatch between assumed and actual clock behavior)

classifications.name: WSL mass-staging of deletions
classifications.determinism_detail: WSL filesystem corruption causes git index to see existing files as absent. Environmental, not reproducible on-demand.
classifications.detection: git status shows 729 deletions for existing files
classifications.fix_type: never use git add -A; always add specific files; spot-check
classifications.jepsen_analog: NONE — hardware/substrate failure, outside Jepsen scope

classifications.name: Git index corruption under concurrent load
classifications.determinism_detail: Index file smaller than expected during high-concurrency commit race. One instance at N≥5.
classifications.jepsen_analog: NONE — index corruption is filesystem failure

classifications.name: Loose object corruption
classifications.determinism_detail: WSL filesystem corruption produces zlib error on commit object. One instance.
classifications.detection: git operations fatal with zlib error
classifications.fix_type: remove corrupt object via reflog, reset to parent SHA
classifications.jepsen_analog: NONE — filesystem corruption

classifications.name: WSL resurrection after compaction
classifications.determinism_detail: WSL resurrects deleted files after context-limit resumption. Deterministic in WSL+compaction+resume scenario.
classifications.detection: git status shows new file for recently deleted path
classifications.fix_type: verify new file against git log before committing
classifications.jepsen_analog: NONE — WSL filesystem behavior

classifications.name: git ls-files transient empty return
classifications.determinism_detail: Transient; resolved by retry. WSL index lock or concurrent writes.
classifications.jepsen_analog: NONE — transient filesystem

gradient_test.in_model_accuracy: 19/19 (100%) — Jepsen 4-layer gradient perfectly predicts swarm bug determinism
gradient_test.out_of_model: 5/24 (21%) bugs in infrastructure layer not covered by Jepsen taxonomy

overall_determinism.jepsen_comparison: Jepsen 60-80% (3-4/5 bugs). Swarm 50-67%. LOWER, not higher.
overall_determinism.prediction_wrong: Predicted swarm determinism > databases due to git consistency. WRONG — infrastructure layer (WSL, 21%) and threshold-based client-server bugs lower the average.

novel_findings.fifth_layer: Infrastructure/substrate layer not in Jepsen taxonomy. Jepsen tests software correctness under partitions; swarm exposes hardware/filesystem bugs that Jepsen assumes away. 21% of swarm failures are in this layer.
novel_findings.threshold_behavior: Swarm client-server bugs have sharp concurrency thresholds (N=3, N=5, N≥8) — below threshold = never, above = always/frequent. Jepsen bugs are probabilistic per-event. This is qualitatively different: swarm failures are phase-transition-like, not probabilistic.
novel_findings.no_byzantine_faults: Git provides linearizable commits. No swarm bug involves conflicting truth or Byzantine disagreement. Entire failure class present in distributed databases is eliminated by consensus substrate (git).
novel_findings.ownership_diffusion: L-561 (lane-closure orphaning) is a novel failure mode: work diffuses across sessions so no single session owns the closing ritual. No Jepsen analog — unique to multi-agent systems with shared ownership.
novel_findings.absorption_as_feature: Commit-by-proxy (N≥5) looks like a bug but functions as gossip protocol. Information propagation via working-tree harvest is an emergent coordination mechanism.

concurrency_thresholds.N1: state-machine bugs only (logic errors independent of concurrency)
concurrency_thresholds.N3: client-server bugs emerge (orient pre-emption, DUE convergence, hot-file contention)
concurrency_thresholds.N5: high-concurrency regime (lesson-slot contention, commit-by-proxy, TTL ghost locks, index corruption)
concurrency_thresholds.N8: extreme regime (staging absorption, commit-by-proxy becomes default pattern)

jepsen_vs_swarm_comparison.jepsen_layers_4: state-machine (100%) > protocol-spec (100% given seq) > client-server (wide timing) > distributed-clock (narrow timing)
jepsen_vs_swarm_comparison.swarm_layers_5: state-machine (100%) > protocol-spec (100% given seq) > client-server (threshold-based) > distributed-clock (TTL mismatch) > infrastructure (substrate)
jepsen_vs_swarm_comparison.key_difference: Jepsen gradient is smooth probability decay. Swarm gradient is CLIFF: 100%/100% → 0%/0%. No intermediate. The cliff is sharper because git eliminates partial-order bugs — failures are either deterministic logic or concurrency races, nothing in between.
jepsen_vs_swarm_comparison.shared_pattern: Architecture layer predicts determinism in both substrates. The gradient transfers across domains (ISO candidate).

prediction_assessment.accuracy_above_70pct: CONFIRMED — 100% within Jepsen layers; 79% including infrastructure (19/24)
prediction_assessment.higher_determinism_than_databases: WRONG — 50-67% vs Jepsen 60-80%. Root cause: infrastructure layer + threshold-based client-server bugs
prediction_assessment.overall: 2/3 predictions confirmed, 1/3 wrong (direction of overall determinism)

iso_candidate: ISO-21: Architecture-layer → determinism gradient transfers from databases (Jepsen) to knowledge systems (swarm). Lower abstraction = more deterministic. Independent of substrate.
next_steps: Test gradient on third substrate (e.g., Kubernetes failure modes, CI/CD pipeline bugs)
Build swarm-internal bug classifier tool that auto-tags new failures by architecture layer
Measure if infrastructure-layer bugs decrease after WSL→native migration
File ISO-21 if gradient holds on third substrate
Connect threshold behavior (N=3/5/8) to F-SP2 throughput ceiling findings
